not_a_crime_scenarios.description: Common situations users report as cyber crimes but are actually user errors, civil disputes, platform policies, or voluntary actions. Smart detection prevents false FIRs and guides users to correct remedies.
not_a_crime_scenarios.purpose: 40% of cyber crime complaints get rejected - we catch these BEFORE filing
not_a_crime_scenarios.detection_priority: Check crime keywords FIRST, then NOT-crime. Only trigger if NO strong crime match.

NAC001_gambling_loss.scenario_hindi: Apni marzi se gambling/betting mein paisa hara

detection_logic.primary_check: Check if user has crime keywords (fraud/scam/cheat/hack) along with gambling keywords

clarifying_question.question: Did you VOLUNTARILY play/bet, OR were you SCAMMED (promised guaranteed wins, then blocked withdrawal)?
options: I played/bet voluntarily - just lost the game
They promised guaranteed wins, took money, then blocked withdrawal/account
They manipulated the game - cards were rigged/fixed
Account frozen after winning - won't let me withdraw

answer_routing.option_2_3_4: IS_CRIME - This is fraud (CT001), not gambling

explanation: Voluntary gambling/betting losses are NOT covered under cyber crime laws.
You willingly risked money in a game of chance. This is not fraud.
Indian law does not protect gambling losses - you accepted the risk.
why_not_crime: ✗ You played voluntarily (not forced or tricked)
✗ Gambling losses ≠ Theft or fraud
✗ You accepted Terms & Conditions (risk disclaimer)
✗ Game outcomes are chance-based (win OR lose)
✗ Platform did not steal - you lost fairly
if_voluntary.legal_status: Many online gambling/rummy apps operate in legal grey area. But losing money in them is NOT a cyber crime - it's a gamble you took.
your_options: 💼 Consumer Court: If platform has technical issues, unfair practices, or ToS violations
🏛️ Civil Court: If amount is very large and you believe rules were violated
💰 Accept the loss: This was a risk you took knowingly
🚫 Stop gambling: Avoid these apps in future to prevent more losses
if_voluntary.what_police_will_say: Cyber Crime Portal and police stations will REJECT this complaint with reason: 'Voluntary gambling loss - not a cyber crime'

consumer_court_process.documents_needed: Transaction proofs, ToS violations proof, platform communication
consumer_court_process.realistic_outcome: Low success rate unless you can prove platform fraud/manipulation

if_scammed.reason: Promising guaranteed wins and blocking withdrawal = Online Financial Fraud

example_rejected_cases.complaint: User filed cyber crime complaint claiming 'money stolen' after losing in rummy games
example_rejected_cases.investigation: Police verified: (1) User voluntarily played 50+ games, (2) User won some games, lost others, (3) Net loss Rs. 45,000, (4) No manipulation detected in algorithm, (5) Clear ToS about gambling risks
example_rejected_cases.outcome: Complaint REJECTED - Reason: 'Voluntary gambling loss, not cyber fraud'
example_rejected_cases.police_statement: User participated in online gambling willingly. Losses in gambling do not constitute theft or fraud under IPC/IT Act.

example_rejected_cases.complaint: User claimed 'scam' after losing money in IPL fantasy league
example_rejected_cases.investigation: User had participated in 100+ contests over 3 months, won Rs. 30,000, lost Rs. 1.2 lakh total. Platform showed all contest rules, entry fees clearly
example_rejected_cases.outcome: REJECTED - 'Voluntary participation in paid gaming contests'
example_rejected_cases.court_note: Fantasy sports involving substantial skill + chance are not prohibited. User accepted financial risk.

NAC001_gambling_loss.public_gaming_act_note: Public Gaming Act 1867 + state amendments govern gambling. Some states ban all online gambling, some allow 'games of skill'. But losing money is NOT theft - it's the nature of gambling.

NAC002_account_frozen_by_platform.scenario_name: Account Suspended/Frozen by Platform for ToS Violation
NAC002_account_frozen_by_platform.scenario_hindi: Platform ne khud account suspend/freeze kiya (rules todne ki wajah se)

clarifying_question.question: Who suspended/froze your account?
options: The platform itself (Instagram/Facebook/etc.) - got email/notification from them
A hacker - unauthorized person changed my password and locked me out
Not sure - account is just not accessible

answer_routing.option_3: Ask follow-up: Did you receive email from platform OR was password changed?

explanation: Platform suspended your account for Terms of Service (ToS) violation.
This is the platform's policy enforcement - NOT a criminal act.
Platforms have the legal right to suspend accounts violating their rules.
why_not_crime: ✗ You agreed to Terms of Service when creating account
✗ Platforms own their service - they can suspend users
✗ This is a civil/contractual matter, not criminal
✗ No theft, fraud, or illegal access occurred
common_violation_reasons: 🚫 Posting prohibited content (violence, hate speech, nudity)
🚫 Copyright infringement (using others' content without permission)
🚫 Spam / Multiple fake accounts / Bot activity
your_options: 📧 Appeal to Platform: Most platforms have appeal process
📝 Review ToS: Understand what rule you may have violated
🆔 Complete Verification: If asking for ID, provide genuine documents
⏳ Wait: Some suspensions are temporary (24 hours - 30 days)
🆕 Create New Account: If permanent ban (but may violate ToS further)
⚖️ Legal Action (rarely successful): Can file case against platform, but very difficult to win

appeal_process.Instagram/Facebook: Help Center → Report a Problem → Appeal suspension

if_platform_action.legal_reality: Platforms (Instagram, Facebook, etc.) are private companies. They can refuse service to anyone for ToS violations. Courts rarely interfere unless discrimination is proven.

if_hacker_action.reason: Unauthorized access by hacker = Account hacking crime

NAC003_wrong_transfer_by_mistake.scenario_name: Money Sent to Wrong Person by User's Own Mistake
NAC003_wrong_transfer_by_mistake.scenario_hindi: Galti se wrong UPI ID / account mein paise transfer kar diye

clarifying_question.question: How did you send money to wrong person?
options: I accidentally typed wrong UPI ID / account number (my mistake)
Someone TRICKED me into sending (fake customer care / police / authority)
Scammer sent fake payment link / QR code and I paid
I was FORCED to send under threat

explanation: Accidentally sending money to wrong UPI ID / account is USER ERROR, not cyber crime.
The recipient did not steal or hack - you voluntarily transferred.
This is a CIVIL matter (contract/unjust enrichment), not criminal.
why_not_crime: ✗ You willingly initiated the transaction
✗ No fraud, deception, or hacking occurred
✗ Recipient didn't trick you - you made typing error
✗ This falls under civil law (unjust enrichment), not cyber crime law
if_user_mistake.legal_category: Civil Wrong - Unjust Enrichment (Indian Contract Act, Sec 70-72)
your_options: 📞 Call Your Bank IMMEDIATELY: Request transaction reversal (works if within 24 hours sometimes)
🏦 Bank Process: File written complaint at bank branch with transaction details
📲 Contact Recipient: If you have their number, request refund politely (save chat proof)
👮 Police Complaint: Can file, but police rarely act on accidental transfers
⚖️ Civil Court: File civil suit for recovery (if amount is large, >Rs. 50,000)
💸 Banking Ombudsman: File complaint if bank refuses to help

bank_reversal_reality.if_within_1_hour: 30-40% chance if bank acts fast + receiving bank cooperates
bank_reversal_reality.upi_specifics: UPI transfers are INSTANT and FINAL. Reversal needs recipient's consent.

if_user_mistake.police_approach: Most police stations will say: 'This is civil matter, not crime. Go to civil court.' Police MAY help if amount is very large (₹1 lakh+) and recipient is uncooperative.

civil_court_option.suit_type: Recovery of money paid by mistake (Order 37 CPC)
civil_court_option.court_fees: Based on amount (e.g., Rs. 5,000 for Rs. 1 lakh claim)

if_user_mistake.prevention_tip: Always verify UPI ID / account number TWICE before sending. Use UPI beneficiary name verification.

if_tricked.reason: Being deceived/tricked into transferring = Online Financial Fraud

NAC004_blocked_on_social_media.scenario_name: Blocked/Unfriended on Social Media by Another User
NAC004_blocked_on_social_media.scenario_hindi: Kisi ne social media pe block kar diya / unfriend kar diya

detection_logic.no_question_needed: This is clearly not a crime - respond immediately

explanation: Someone blocking/unfriending you on social media is NOT a cyber crime.
People have the fundamental right to choose who they interact with.
Blocking is a privacy feature - it's not illegal.
why_not_crime: ✗ Everyone has right to block anyone (freedom of association)
✗ No theft, fraud, or illegal access occurred
✗ Social media interaction is voluntary, not mandatory
✗ Blocking is a platform feature for user safety/privacy
response.legal_reality: Indian law does NOT recognize 'right to be friends' or 'right to access someone's social media'. Blocking is legal and within user's rights.
response.exceptions: ONLY a crime if there was harassment, threats, or blackmail BEFORE the blocking. Otherwise, blocking itself is legal.
what_to_do: Do NOT create fake accounts to contact them (that's harassment)
Do NOT send messages through friends (that's stalking behavior)
If there's a genuine issue, resolve through mutual friends/family
response.if_harassment_involved: If they blocked you AFTER you harassed/threatened them, YOU may be committing a crime (cyber stalking, harassment). If they harassed YOU and then blocked, that harassment may be a crime - but blocking itself isn't.

NAC005_bad_customer_service.scenario_name: Poor Customer Service / Delayed Refund / Product Not Delivered
NAC005_bad_customer_service.scenario_hindi: Company ka kharab service / refund nahi mil raha / saman nahi aaya

clarifying_question.question: Is this a SERVICE/REFUND issue from LEGITIMATE company, OR complete SCAM/FRAUD (fake company)?
options: Legitimate company (Amazon, Flipkart, etc.) but bad service / refund delay
Small company/website - bad service but company exists
Complete SCAM - fake website, took money, disappeared / no company exists
Company exists but CHEATED me - sold fake/counterfeit product intentionally

answer_routing.option_4: CIVIL + CONSUMER - Not cyber crime, but consumer case + possible fraud case

explanation: Bad customer service / delayed refund from LEGITIMATE companies = CONSUMER COMPLAINT, not cyber crime.
This is a business dispute / contractual issue, not criminal fraud.
Cyber Crime Portal is for CRIMES, not business/service disputes.
why_not_crime: ✗ Company exists (not a fake/scam website)
✗ Delay ≠ Fraud (delays happen, frustrating but not criminal)
✗ This is breach of contract / consumer rights, not IT Act violation
✗ You made a legitimate purchase - company is just slow/poor service
your_options: 📧 Escalate within Company: Email to grievance officer / CEO / social media
🌐 National Consumer Helpline: 1915 (toll-free) or consumerhelpline.gov.in
💼 Consumer Court: File online at edaakhil.nic.in (e-Daakhil portal)
📱 Social Media Pressure: Tweet/post complaint - companies often respond faster
⚖️ Legal Notice: Send through lawyer if amount is significant
💳 Credit Card Chargeback: If paid by card, dispute with card company

consumer_court_process.filing_fee: Varies by claim amount (Rs. 200 to Rs. 5,000)
documents_needed: Company communication (emails, chats, customer care recording)
consumer_court_process.timeline: 3-6 months for simple cases, 1-2 years for complex
consumer_court_process.lawyer_needed: Optional for amounts below Rs. 10 lakh
consumer_court_process.success_rate: 70-80% for clear cases with good documentation

escalation_emails.note: CC your complaint to grievance officer + social media handles

if_legitimate_company.social_media_strategy: Tag company's official handle on Twitter/X with polite but firm complaint. Public visibility often gets faster response.

if_fake_scam_website.reason: Fake website that took money and disappeared = Online Financial Fraud
